beats.description: A crew member hauls the extension ladder off the truck and sets its feet on the wet floor by the loading door - no mat, no footing check, the lean far steeper than four to one.
beats.heading: EXT. YARD / INT. LOADING DOOR - MORNING
beats.vo: The job was fifteen minutes. The setup took ninety seconds too few.

beats.description: Three rungs from the top he leans into an overreach, buckle past the rail, fingers stretching for the junction box. The feet lose grip on the wet concrete and the ladder slides out from under him.
beats.heading: INT. LOADING DOOR, TWELVE FEET UP - CONTINUOUS
beats.vo: Overreach is a decision. It just never feels like one.

beats.description: Corrective replay: dry footing, a proper four-to-one angle, three points of contact all the way up, tie off at the top. The same job, done in sixteen minutes, done once.
beats.heading: INT. LOADING DOOR - REPLAY
beats.vo: One minute of setup buys you the whole way down.

logline: A wet floor, a steep lean, and one overreach turn a junction-box fix into a twelve-foot fall.
rule_card: Set the angle. Dry the feet. Keep three points of contact - and never overreach.
title: Four to One
